title: uxheaderfooter
html:
uxheaderfooter

Add to My Topics

  • Last UpdatedApr 29, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The uxheaderfooter widget is a component-level widget to structure web page layouts into three semantic regions — header, main, and footer. The uxheaderfooter widget supports two configuration modes: a smart index‑based assignment that automatically maps children to header, main, and footer based on their order, and a name‑based assignment where developers explicitly place widgets using slot labels. The first mode offers effortless defaults for quick setups, while the second one provides precise, declarative control with warnings for invalid slot names.

Default Logical Widget

The default logical widgets is:
  • ux-HeaderFooter

Configuration

The component widget can have any number of child widgets and there are two ways available to decide which section a particular child widget belongs to, an index-based assignment and using a 'slot' property on the child widgets.

Index based assignment

This is the default assignment if you have not specified any slot or section for any of the child widgets:

  • If there is only one child widget, it is assigned to the main section.
  • If there are two child widgets, the first one is assigned to header section and second to the main.
  • If there are three or more child widgets, the first one is assigned to the header section, last one to the footer section, and everything in between to the main section.

Name-based assignment

In a name-based assignment, you can decide which of the child widgets are part of header, main or footer sections by specifying the 'slot' property on the child widgets. All the widgets belonging to a certain slot, such as header, main, or footer, are 'slotted' into the corresponding section. Name-based assignment is enabled only when at least one child widget has the slot property defined. Otherwise, index-based assignment is used.

In case the slot specification is not specified, the child widget are slotted into the main section.

In case the slot specification is incorrect, that is unknown slot name, a warning is shown at run-time in the browser's console log and the child widget is slotted into main section.

Keyboard support

The uxheaderfooter widget is a container and does not support any keyboard navigation by itself.

You can use the Tab key to move through the child widgets inside the uxheaderfooter container, as long as those widgets support keyboard navigation. The uxheaderfooter widget itself is not focusable.

Layout processing

There is an outer container inside which three child elements are created for each of the sections.

Each section uses a custom web component, such as uf-shell, uf-header, uf-main, uf-footer built with the Fast Framework, ensuring efficient rendering and integration with Fluent UI.

The resulting DOM structure looks like the following:

<!– Root element →
 <uf-shell class="u-header-footer">
      <!– Header →
        <uf-header class="u-header">
          <!– Header content here →
        </uf-header>  

      <!– Main →    
        <uf-main class="u-main">
          <!– Main content here →
        </uf-main>     

      <!– Footer →
        <uf-footer class="u-footer">
          <!– Footer content here →
        </uf-footer>
  </uf-shell>
Note: For accessibility with screen readers, we have provided role attributes as follows:
  • header - banner
  • main - main
  • footer - contentinfo

Widget properties

The uxheaderfooter widget supports the following properties:
Table 1. Common Widget Properties
Property Description
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes the class specified by the classname.
Table 2. Child Widget Properties
Property Description
area-slot Explicitly assign widgets to specific slots. The options are:
  • header
  • main (default)
  • footer
Table 3. Widget Header Section Properties
Property Description
header:placement Specifies the placement for the header section. The options are:
  • hidden: The header section is hidden from viewing.
  • scroll : The header is positioned according to the normal document flow and scrolls with the page.
  • sticky (default): The header remains fixed to the top of the viewport.
header:layout-type Controls how child entities are laid out in the header slot and how overflow is handled. The available options are:
  • horizontal-scroll : Horizontal layout with scroll overflow.
  • horizontal-wrap (default): Horizontal layout that wraps into multiple lines.
  • vertical-scroll: Vertical layout with scroll overflow
  • vertical-wrap: Vertical layout that wraps into multiple columns.
  • auto: Uses the effective layout behavior from the nearest ancestor.
header:horizontal-align Controls how child widget entities in the header slot are positioned along the horizontal direction (reversed in RTL) when not overflowing. If the layout is horizontal, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is vertical, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Left align child widgets.
  • center: Center align child widgets.
  • end: Right align child widgets.
  • space-between (default): Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch child widgets to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, stretch behavior is not supported for the following widgets:
    • uxCheckbox
    • uxSwitch
    • uxRadioGroup
    • uxPlainText
  • auto: Uses the effective horizontal alignment from the nearest ancestor.
header:vertical-align Controls how child widget entities in the header slot are positioned along the vertical direction when not overflowing. If the layout is vertical, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is horizontal, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Align to top.
  • center(default): Align to center.
  • end: Align to bottom.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch items to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, only uxtextarea supports vertical stretch.
  • auto: Uses the effective vertical alignment from the nearest ancestor.
Table 4. Widget Main Section Properties
Property Description
main:layout-type Controls how child widget entities are laid out in the main slot and how overflow is handled. The available options are:
  • horizontal-scroll : Horizontal layout with scroll overflow.
  • horizontal-wrap: Horizontal layout that wraps into multiple lines.
  • vertical-scroll (default): Vertical layout with scroll overflow
  • vertical-wrap: Vertical layout that wraps into multiple columns.
  • auto: Uses the effective layout behavior from the nearest ancestor.
main:horizontal-align Controls how child widget entities in the main slot are positioned along the horizontal direction (reversed in RTL) when not overflowing. If the layout is horizontal, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is vertical, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start (default): Left align child widgets.
  • center: Center align child widgets.
  • end: Right align child widgets.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch child widgets to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, stretch behavior is not supported for the following widgets:
    • uxCheckbox
    • uxSwitch
    • uxRadioGroup
    • uxPlainText
  • auto: Uses the effective horizontal alignment from the nearest ancestor.
main:vertical-align Controls how child widget entities in the main slot are positioned along the vertical direction when not overflowing. If the layout is vertical, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is horizontal, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start (default): Align to top.
  • center: Align to center.
  • end: Align to bottom.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch items to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, only uxtextarea supports vertical stretch.
  • auto: Uses the effective vertical alignment from the nearest ancestor.
Table 5. Widget Footer Section Properties
Property Description
footer:placement Specifies the placement for the footer section. The options are:
  • hidden: The footer section is hidden from viewing.
  • scroll : The footer is positioned according to the normal document flow and scrolls with the page.
  • sticky (default): The footer remains fixed to the top of the viewport.
footer:layout-type Controls how child entities are laid out in the footer slot and how overflow is handled. The available options are:
  • horizontal-scroll : Horizontal layout with scroll overflow.
  • horizontal-wrap (default): Horizontal layout that wraps into multiple lines.
  • vertical-scroll: Vertical layout with scroll overflow
  • vertical-wrap: Vertical layout that wraps into multiple columns.
  • auto: Uses the effective layout behavior from the nearest ancestor.
footer:horizontal-align Controls how child widget entities in the footer slot are positioned along the horizontal direction (reversed in RTL) when not overflowing. If the layout is horizontal, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is vertical, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Left align child widgets.
  • center: Center align child widgets.
  • end: Right align child widgets.
  • space-between (default): Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch child widgets to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, stretch behavior is not supported for the following widgets:
    • uxCheckbox
    • uxSwitch
    • uxRadioGroup
    • uxPlainText
  • auto: Uses the effective horizontal alignment from the nearest ancestor.
footer:vertical-align Controls how child widget entities in the footer slot are positioned along the vertical direction when not overflowing. If the layout is vertical, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is horizontal, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Align to top.
  • center(default): Align to center.
  • end: Align to bottom.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch items to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, only uxtextarea supports vertical stretch.
  • auto: Uses the effective vertical alignment from the nearest ancestor.
Note: We provide a default theme with the ux-HeaderFooter widget, and all ux-widgets automatically adapt to this theme. Additionally, we apply a default density to the component to ensure consistent and appropriate spacing within and around its three sections.

UI blocking

None
Note:

As the widget is not interactive, no UI blocking specific to the widget is applied.

Widget styling

CSS Selector Selected node(s)
.u-header-footer The widget node
.u-header The header node
.u-main The main node
.u-footer The footer node

Widget sources

The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/header_footer .
